definition: A heart rate that exceeds the normal resting rate, typically over 100 beats per minute.
A slower than normal heart rate, typically fewer than 60 beats per minute.
Difficult or labored breathing; shortness of breath.
A bluish discoloration of the skin resulting from poor circulation or inadequate oxygenation of the blood.
A condition in which the body or a region of the body is deprived of adequate oxygen supply at the tissue level.
Swelling caused by excess fluid trapped in your body's tissues.
The action of listening to sounds from the heart, lungs, or other organs, typically with a stethoscope.
A method of feeling with the fingers or hands during a physical examination.
The likely course of a disease or ailment.
The cause, set of causes, or manner of causation of a disease or condition.
The manner of development of a disease.
Relating to or denoting any disease or condition which arises spontaneously or for which the cause is unknown.
Relating to illness caused by medical examination or treatment.
Originating or taking place in a hospital.
Having or showing the symptoms of a fever.
